title: Variables should be initialized before use
description: Variables should be initialized before use. Analyze your code intentionality-logical reliability. Bug cwe symbolic-execution based-on-misra. Variables should be initialized before their use to avoid unexpected behavior due to garbage values. Why is this an issue? How can I fix it? More Info: A local variable of any built-in type (such as int, float, and pointers), declared without an initial value is not initialized to any particular value. Consequently, if no value is assigned to such a variable first, the code that uses it has no defined behavior. Similarly, structures that simply aggregate variables of built-in types are not initialized.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant: int addition() {   int x; // Noncompliant: x is not initialized   return x + 10; // x has garbage value }  int dereference() {   int* p; // Noncompliant: p is not initialized   return *p; // p has garbage value }  void useArray() {   int arr[5]; // Noncompliant: array not initialized   printf("%d", arr[0]); // garbage value }
compliant: int addition() {   int x = 0; // Compliant: x is initialized   return x + 10; }  int dereference() {   int value = 42;   int* p = &value; // Compliant: p is initialized   return *p; }  void useArray() {   int arr[5] = {0}; // Compliant: array initialized   printf("%d", arr[0]); }

checks.message: Variables should be initialized before use. Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.
